title: Living in Boulder vlog 9 - 22 - 2018: US Law Code

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night/early-morning update after a week away from videos. He describes his ongoing project of formatting the US Law Code (and other legal documents like the Colorado Constitution and Boulder City Charter) into a readable, text-to-speech-friendly book format on his website. He also recounts a neighborhood incident involving a screaming woman and a suspicious car, shares a phone repair saga, and briefly muses on capitalism, greed, and his theory that rewarding non-monetary effort could fix societal problems.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.topic: US Law Code as readable book
stances.position: Worth doing — stripping extraneous content and making it text-to-speech friendly is the best way to learn it
The fix is rewarding people for hard work and knowledge rather than money-making, which redirects energy toward improving the world
Addicted but not concerned — notes he has lost weight
Uses it but acknowledges it can cause mistakes when working; prefers medical-grade
